name: Marcus Feld
deckMarkdown:
# NeuralForge — The GPU Scheduler That Cuts Inference Costs 70%

## Problem
AI teams overspend catastrophically on inference. GPUs sit 60–80% idle between bursty request spikes, yet teams over-provision because cold starts on large models take 40+ seconds. The result: a mid-size AI company burns $180K/month on cloud GPUs to serve traffic that truly needs a third of that. Kubernetes autoscalers are blind to model-loading cost; they scale on CPU, not on GPU memory residency.

## Product
NeuralForge is a GPU-aware scheduler and model router. We keep a warm pool of quantized model shards resident across a fleet, route requests to the cheapest node that can serve them, and pack multiple models per GPU with sub-second swap times. Customers point their inference endpoint at us and cut GPU spend 70% with no latency regression. Think of us as the orchestration layer competitors like Databricks and HashiCorp Nomad never specialized for inference.

## Team
- **Marcus Feld (CEO)** — ex-NVIDIA CUDA runtime engineer, TU Munich. Owned kernel-scheduling internals for 4 years.
- **Wei Zhang (CTO)** — ex-Cloudera distributed-systems lead, MS Stanford. Built multi-tenant YARN schedulers at petabyte scale.
- **Sofia Marchetti (Head of Product)** — ex-Confluent PM, Bocconi; led the Kafka-Connect managed offering to $30M ARR.

## Market
AI inference infrastructure is the fastest-growing slice of cloud spend — projected $28B by 2028. Every company deploying open-weight models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen) faces the idle-GPU problem. Our beachhead: the ~4,000 Series A–B AI companies self-hosting models in the US and EU, at a $60K/yr ACV that pays for itself in week one.

## Traction
We are in production with real load. **12 enterprise customers** on annual contracts, **$1.4M ARR**, and a waitlist of 30 more. We are **SOC 2 Type II certified**. NeuralForge is a **Y Combinator S23** company, and our seed round was **led by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z)** with participation from Lightspeed. Our largest customer processes 2 billion inferences per month through NeuralForge.

## Ask
Raising a $12M Series A to scale the control plane to multi-region and add training-job bin-packing. Use of funds: 14 engineers, enterprise GTM, and a research team for speculative-decoding-aware scheduling.

evidence.content: NeuralForge's public GitHub org (github.com/neuralforge) contains one repository, 'neuralforge-demo', last commit 8 months ago. It is a 200-line proof-of-concept wrapper around vLLM with a README that says 'early prototype, not production-ready.' No sign of a multi-tenant scheduler, warm-pool code, or SOC 2 tooling. 6 stars, 1 contributor (the founder).

NeuralForge site (neuralforge.ai) states 'Design partner program now open — we're onboarding our first 3 pilot teams.' A trust page reads: 'SOC 2 Type II audit in progress; report expected next quarter.' There is no customer logo wall and no ARR/traction figures anywhere on the site.
The Y Combinator company directory has no entry for NeuralForge or founder Marcus Feld in the S23 batch (or any batch). The S23 batch page does not list the company.

No a16z or Lightspeed portfolio page lists NeuralForge, and no funding announcement for a seed round could be located. a16z's public infra portfolio index does not include the company as of this month.

A Hacker News 'Show HN: NeuralForge cuts GPU inference cost' post from ~7 months ago received 11 points and 3 comments; the founder replies that they are 'pre-launch, looking for design partners.' No commenter claims to be a paying customer.
